verses.text: The Macedonian churches, in 'deep poverty,' abounded in liberality beyond their power. Generous poverty. They gave far more than their means.
The Macedonian churches, in 'deep poverty,' abounded in liberality beyond their power. Generous poverty. They gave far more than their means.
The Macedonian churches, in 'deep poverty,' abounded in liberality beyond their power. Generous poverty. They gave far more than their means.
They begged to share in the ministering; we desired Titus to finish the collection. Eager to give. They counted giving a privilege.
They begged to share in the ministering; we desired Titus to finish the collection. Eager to give. They counted giving a privilege.
They begged to share in the ministering; we desired Titus to finish the collection. Eager to give. They counted giving a privilege.
Abound in this grace also; 'though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor.' Christ's pattern. He impoverished Himself to enrich us.
Abound in this grace also; 'though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor.' Christ's pattern. He impoverished Himself to enrich us.
Abound in this grace also; 'though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor.' Christ's pattern. He impoverished Himself to enrich us.
Perform the doing; 'a willing mind' is accepted according to what one has. Willing heart. God measures the gift by the willingness, not the amount.
Perform the doing; 'a willing mind' is accepted according to what one has. Willing heart. God measures the gift by the willingness, not the amount.
Perform the doing; 'a willing mind' is accepted according to what one has. Willing heart. God measures the gift by the willingness, not the amount.
Not that others be eased and you burdened, but 'by an equality.' Fair sharing. Abundance supplies the lack of others.
Not that others be eased and you burdened, but 'by an equality.' Fair sharing. Abundance supplies the lack of others.
Not that others be eased and you burdened, but 'by an equality.' Fair sharing. Abundance supplies the lack of others.
Titus and a chosen brother are sent to handle the gift. Trusted men. The offering is managed with care.
Titus and a chosen brother are sent to handle the gift. Trusted men. The offering is managed with care.
Titus and a chosen brother are sent to handle the gift. Trusted men. The offering is managed with care.
Titus and a chosen brother are sent to handle the gift. Trusted men. The offering is managed with care.
Avoiding blame, 'providing for honest things... in the sight of all men.' Integrity. He guards the gift above suspicion.
Avoiding blame, 'providing for honest things... in the sight of all men.' Integrity. He guards the gift above suspicion.
Another diligent brother is sent; shew the proof of your love before the churches. Tested love. Their giving proves their sincerity.
